questions.question: Which century marks the beginning of new religious and social movements like Buddhism and Jainism in the Gangetic valley?
questions.explanation: The sixth century BCE marks the beginning of new religious and social movements in the Gangetic valley in the form of Buddhism and Jainism.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Why did Buddhism and Jainism become highly popular during their early stages?
options: They were supported by foreign invaders
They opposed the varna and jati systems of the Hindu religion
They promoted complex rituals and sacrifices
They were the only religions allowed by the kings
questions.explanation: Both religions became popular as they opposed the varna and jati systems of the Hindu religion.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which kingdom emerged as powerful and consolidated its control over other regions during this period?
questions.explanation: Magadha emerged as a powerful kingdom and consolidated its control over the other regions.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: By which century did the Mauryas establish their power?
questions.explanation: By the fourth century BCE the Mauryas established their power.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Who emerged as the most powerful king of the Mauryan dynasty and patronised the Buddhist shraman tradition?
questions.explanation: Ashoka emerged as the most powerful king of the Mauryan dynasty who patronised the Buddhist shraman tradition in the third century BCE.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Apart from Buddhism and Jainism, what other forms of worship were prevalent during this time?
questions.explanation: Worship of Yakshas and mother-goddesses were prevalent during that time.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How are the Mauryan pillars fundamentally different from the Achamenian pillars?
options: Mauryan pillars are made of wood, Achamenian of stone
Mauryan pillars are constructed in pieces, Achamenian are rock-cut
Mauryan pillars are rock-cut, Achamenian are constructed in pieces by a mason
There is no structural difference between them
questions.explanation: The Mauryan pillars are rock-cut pillars thus displaying the carver's skills, whereas the Achamenian pillars are constructed in pieces by a mason.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In which part of the Mauryan Empire were the stone pillars erected by Ashoka primarily found?
questions.explanation: Stone pillars were erected by Ashoka, which have been found in the north Indian part of the Mauryan Empire.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What kind of decoration is typically found on the abacuses of Mauryan pillars?
questions.explanation: Abacuses are decorated with stylised lotuses.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which of the following locations in Bihar has existing pillars with capital figures?
questions.explanation: Some of the existing pillars with capital figures were found at Basarah-Bakhira, Lauriya-Nandangarh and Rampurva in Bihar.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which Mauryan pillar capital is considered the finest example of their sculptural tradition?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan pillar capital found at Sarnath popularly known as the Lion Capital is the finest example of Mauryan sculptural tradition.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What great historical event in the life of the Buddha does the Lion Capital at Sarnath symbolise?
questions.explanation: This pillar capital symbolising Dhammachakrapravartana (the first sermon by the Buddha) has become a standard symbol of this great historical event.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: At which of the following places have large statues of Yakshas and Yakhinis been found?
questions.explanation: Large statues of Yakshas and Yakhinis are found at many places like Patna, Vidisha and Mathura.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is one of the distinguishing elements present in all the monumental Yaksha and Yakhini images?
options: They are depicted in a seated position
questions.explanation: These monumental images are mostly in the standing position. One of the distinguishing elements in all these images is their polished surface.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where was one of the finest examples of a Yakshi figure, described as tall and well-built, discovered?
questions.explanation: One of the finest examples is a Yakshi figure from Didarganj, Patna, which is tall and well-built.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where is the monumental rock-cut elephant that shows modelling in round with linear rhythm located?
questions.explanation: Depiction of a monumental rock-cut elephant at Dhauli in Odisha shows modelling in round with linear rhythm.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is the name of the rock-cut cave carved at the Barabar hills near Gaya in Bihar?
questions.explanation: The rock-cut cave carved at Barabar hills near Gaya in Bihar is known as the Lomus Rishi cave.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How is the facade of the Lomus Rishi cave decorated at its entrance?
questions.explanation: The facade of the cave is decorated with the semicircular chaitya arch as the entrance.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which animal is depicted in a frieze carved in high relief on the chaitya arch of the Lomus Rishi cave?
questions.explanation: The elephant frieze carved in high relief on the chaitya arch shows considerable movement.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: To which sect did King Ashoka donate the Lomus Rishi cave?
questions.explanation: The cave was donated by Ashoka for the Ajivika sect.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In which modern state were the stupas over the relics of the Buddha at Rajagraha, Vaishali, Vethadipa, and Pava constructed?
questions.explanation: It is important to note that the stupas were constructed over the relics of the Buddha at Rajagraha, Vaishali, Vethadipa and Pava in Bihar.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which set of locations for Buddha's relic stupas is situated in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Stupas were constructed over the relics of the Buddha at... Kapilavastu, Allakappa and Ramagrama in Nepal.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Kushinagar and Pippalvina, locations of stupas built over the Buddha's relics, are in which region?
questions.explanation: Stupas were constructed over the relics of the Buddha at... Kushinagar and Pippalvina in Uttar Pradesh.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where is a notable example of a third century BCE stupa structure located in Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: One of the examples of the structure of a stupa in the third century BCE is at Bairat in Rajasthan.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What material was originally used to build the great stupa at Sanchi during the time of Ashoka?
questions.explanation: The great stupa at Sanchi... was built with bricks during the time of Ashoka and later it was covered with stone and many new additions were made.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: From which century onwards do we find many inscriptional evidences mentioning donors and their professions?
questions.explanation: From the second century BCE onwards, we get many inscriptional evidences mentioning donors and, at times, their profession.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What was the dominant pattern of patronage for these monuments?
options: Solely funded by the artists themselves
questions.explanation: The pattern of patronage has been a very collective one and there are very few examples of royal patronage.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: At which site is the artisan Kanha mentioned in an inscription?
questions.explanation: However, there are very few inscriptions mentioning the names of artisans such as Kanha at Pitalkhora.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where is the artisan Balaka, disciple of Kanha, mentioned in an inscription?
questions.explanation: There are very few inscriptions mentioning the names of artisans such as Kanha at Pitalkhora and his disciple Balaka at Kondane caves in Maharashtra.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How did traders typically record their donations?
options: Along with their place of origin
By mentioning the total cost of the donation
questions.explanation: Traders recorded their donation along with their place of origin.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How many component parts did the Sarnath Lion Capital originally consist of?
questions.explanation: The capital originally consisted of five component parts: (i) the shaft... (ii) a lotus bell base... (iii) a drum... (iv) four majestic addorsed lions, and (v) the crowning element, Dharamchakra.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In which direction do the four animals proceed on the drum of the Lion Capital?
questions.explanation: The capital originally consisted of... a drum on the bell base with four animals proceeding clockwise.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What was the original crowning element of the Sarnath Lion Capital?
questions.explanation: The capital originally consisted of... the crowning element, Dharamchakra, a large wheel, was also a part of this pillar.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which parts of the Sarnath Lion Capital were omitted when it was adopted as the National Emblem of India?
options: The crowning wheel and the lotus base
questions.explanation: The capital without the crowning wheel and the lotus base has been adopted as the National Emblem of Independent India.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What specific facial feature of the lions on the capital shows the sculptor's observation for naturalistic depiction?
options: The inversed lines of the lips and their projection
The geometric shape of the ears
questions.explanation: The inversed lines of the lips and its subsequent effect of projection at the end of the lips show the sculptor's observation for naturalistic depiction.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which of the following is a typical feature of the Mauryan Period surface treatment, as seen on the Lion Capital?
options: It is left rough and unhewn
It is painted in bright colors
questions.explanation: The surface of the sculpture is heavily polished which is typical of the Mauryan Period.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How many spokes does the chakra (wheel) on the abacus of the Lion Capital have?
questions.explanation: The abacus has the depiction of a chakra (wheel) having twenty-four spokes in all the four directions.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which four animals are finely carved between every chakra on the abacus of the Lion Capital?
questions.explanation: The abacus has the depiction of a chakra... and a bull, a horse, an elephant and a lion between every chakra is finely carved.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What supports the circular abacus of the Lion Capital?
questions.explanation: The circular abacus is supported by an inverted lotus capital.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is the condition of the lion capital found at Sanchi?
options: It is in a dilapidated condition
It is larger than the Sarnath capital
questions.explanation: A lion capital has also been found at Sanchi but is in a dilapidated condition.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What object is the life-size standing image of the Yakshini from Didargunj holding?
questions.explanation: The life-size standing image of a Yakshini holding a chauri (flywhisk) from Didargunj near modern Patna is another good example.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In the Didargunj Yakshini sculpture, which hand is holding the chauri?
options: It rests on her shoulder unheld
questions.explanation: The chauri is held in the right hand whereas the left hand is broken.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What sculptural technique creates the effect of a bulging belly in the Didargunj Yakshini image?
options: The tightening of the garment around the belly
Polishing the stomach area more than the rest
questions.explanation: The tightening of garment around the belly creates the effect of a bulging belly.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What ornaments adorn the feet of the Didargunj Yakshini?
questions.explanation: The middle band of the garment falls till the feet. Thick bell-ornaments adorn the feet.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How is the hair of the Didargunj Yakshini styled at the back?
options: Tied in a knot at the back
questions.explanation: The back is equally impressive. The hair is tied in a knot at the back. The back is bare.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which of the following describes the consistent components of a stupa?
options: A square base and a pyramid top
A cylindrical drum, a circular anda with a harmika and chhatra on top
A hollow cave with a flat roof
A tall column topped with an animal figure
questions.explanation: The stupa consists of a cylindrical drum and a circular anda with a harmika and chhatra on the top which remain consistent throughout.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How was the Buddha depicted during the early phase of Buddhism?
options: Symbolically through footprints, stupas, lotus throne, chakra
questions.explanation: During the early phase of Buddhism, Buddha is depicted symbolically through footprints, stupas, lotus throne, chakra, etc.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which forms of narrative are used in the Buddhist pictorial tradition to depict stories?
questions.explanation: Mainly synoptic narrative, continuous narrative and episodic narrative are used in the pictorial tradition.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is the event of the Buddha's liberation from the cycle of birth called?
questions.explanation: The main events associated with the Buddha's life which were frequently depicted were events related to the birth... and mahaparinibbana (liberation from the cycle of birth).
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Jataka story frequently depicted on stupa decorations?
questions.explanation: Among the Jataka stories that are frequently depicted are Chhadanta Jataka, Vidurpundita Jataka, Ruru Jataka, Sibi Jataka, Vessantara Jataka and Shama Jataka.
questions.subject: Fine Arts
